title: crocanthemum glomeratum
intro: Crocanthemum glomeratum, also known as tickseed or roundleaf croton, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, and can also be made into salves and creams to topically apply its medicinal benefits.
